title: Wire EventClipPlayer + ?clip=inline opt-in into CameraEventDetailPage (Phase 2)
description:
Phase 2 integration step. Distinct from ce-6pr (Phase 1 bbox wiring).

## Behavior
- useUrlFlag('clip', 'inline') gates whether EventClipPlayer renders at all.
- When flag is on AND event.has_clip, render EventClipPlayer above the snapshot block.
- Player + snapshot both visible when both clip and snapshot exist (per layout decision).
- Player onError → fallback message visible, snapshot/InfoCards/favorite still render.

## TDD
RED:
- ?clip=inline + has_clip=true → video present, before snapshot in DOM order.
- No ?clip=inline → no video regardless of has_clip.
- has_clip=true, has_snapshot=false, ?clip=inline → video present, no snapshot.
- video onError → fallback shown, snapshot/InfoCards/favorite still rendered.
GREEN: implement.

## Depends on
- ce-xf0 (EventClipPlayer with onError fallback)
- ce-j2v (clip proxy)
- new useUrlFlag task

## Spec
- Phase 2 scenarios: 'Player renders inline', 'Player hidden by default', 'Phase 2 opt-in flag', 'Clip fails to load — fallback renders', 'Event has clip but no snapshot'
- AC17, AC21
- docs/specs/inline-event-clip-playback.md

title: Add useUrlFlag SSR-safe hook for ?clip=inline opt-in (Phase 2)
description:
New hook that reads a URL query param flag safely under SSR.

## Signature
useUrlFlag(key: string, value: string): boolean

## Behavior
- Returns false during SSR (no window access during render).
- Returns true when window.location's URL has ?{key}={value}.
- Reactively updates on popstate / router navigation.

## TDD
RED: hook tests cover server (false), missing param (false), wrong value (false), match (true), URL change updates the value.
GREEN: useEffect-based implementation with URL constructor.

## Files
- src/features/camera-details/hooks/useUrlFlag.ts (new)
- src/features/camera-details/hooks/useUrlFlag.test.ts (new)

## Spec
- AC21
- docs/specs/inline-event-clip-playback.md > 'Phased rollout' and 'Constraints' bullet about useUrlFlag

title: Bun-runtime streaming smoke test (Phase 2 prerequisite)
description:
Manual verification step that validates getEventClipStream's Range + Content-Length behavior survives the Bun/Nitro runtime end-to-end BEFORE the clip proxy refactor (ce-j2v) commits.

## Procedure
1. Spin up dev server (bun run dev).
2. Spin up a minimal scratch route returning getEventClipStream directly.
3. curl with Range: bytes=0-1023 against /api/events/{id}/clip.
4. Assert: 206 status, Content-Range and Content-Length: 1024 headers, body length is 1024 bytes.
5. curl without Range. Assert: 200 with Accept-Ranges: bytes and Content-Length matching full mp4.

## Outcome
- PASS: continue with ce-j2v as planned.
- FAIL: fall back to synthetic Range slicing on a buffered response. Update Phase 2 plan before continuing.

Record curl invocations and outputs in a comment on this task.

## Spec
- AC22
- docs/specs/inline-event-clip-playback.md > 'Constraints' bullet about Content-Length / Range

title: Wire bounding-box toggle into CameraEventDetailPage (Phase 1)
description:
Integrate the new pieces into the event detail page.

## Behavior
1. When event.has_clip, render EventClipPlayer above the snapshot block.
2. Add a 'Show detection box' pill button immediately above the snapshot, but only when isNonZeroBox(event.box ?? event.data.box) returns true.
3. Manage showBoundingBox via useState(false). Pass into EventSnapshot and SnapshotLightbox.
4. Button uses aria-pressed for accessibility.

## Files
- src/features/camera-details/components/CameraEventDetailPage.tsx
- src/features/camera-details/components/CameraEventDetailPage.test.tsx

## TDD
RED:
- Event with has_clip=true → video element present, appears above snapshot in DOM order.
- Event with has_clip=false → no video element.
- Event with non-zero box → toggle button is in DOM with aria-pressed='false' initially.
- Event with zero/null box → no toggle button.
- Clicking toggle flips aria-pressed and updates EventSnapshot src.
GREEN: Implement.

## Depends on
- ce-s8a (isNonZeroBox)
- ce-xf0 (EventClipPlayer)
- ce-36m (snapshot/lightbox bbox prop)

## Acceptance
- Spec scenarios: 'Player renders inline', 'Bounding-box overlay toggle', 'Toggle hidden when no detection box exists', 'Lightbox respects overlay state'
- Spec AC6, AC7, AC12
- Spec ref: docs/specs/inline-event-clip-playback.md

title: Add showBoundingBox prop to EventSnapshot and SnapshotLightbox
description:
Both components currently render a hard-coded src. Add an optional showBoundingBox: boolean prop; when true, append ?bbox=true to the snapshot URL.

## Files
- src/features/camera-details/components/EventSnapshot.tsx
- src/features/camera-details/components/SnapshotLightbox.tsx
- corresponding *.test.tsx files

## TDD
RED:
- EventSnapshot test: prop=false → src is /api/events/{id}/snapshot. prop=true → src is /api/events/{id}/snapshot?bbox=true.
- SnapshotLightbox test: same behavior.
GREEN: Implement.

## Acceptance
- Spec scenarios: 'Bounding-box overlay toggle', 'Lightbox zoom respects current overlay state'
- Spec AC6, AC12
- Spec ref: docs/specs/inline-event-clip-playback.md

title: Add EventClipPlayer component with onError fallback (Phase 2)
description:
New React component that renders a native HTML5 video element for the proxied event clip.

## Component
```tsx
<video
  src={`/api/events/${eventId}/clip`}
  controls
  playsInline
  preload="metadata"
  className="..."
/>
```
Inside an aspect-ratio container so the layout doesn't shift while metadata loads.

## Files
- src/features/camera-details/components/EventClipPlayer.tsx (new)
- src/features/camera-details/components/EventClipPlayer.test.tsx (new)

## TDD
RED: Render test asserts the rendered element has playsInline, controls, preload='metadata', and the correct src. No autoplay attribute.
GREEN: Implement.

## Acceptance
- Spec scenarios: 'Event has a clip — player renders inline', 'Event has clip but no snapshot'
- Spec ref: docs/specs/inline-event-clip-playback.md

title: Add isNonZeroBox helper for detection box presence
description:
Pure utility that returns true when a BoundingBox has at least one non-zero coordinate. Used by the event detail page to decide whether to render the bounding-box toggle.

## Files
- src/features/camera-details/utils/boundingBox.ts (new)
- src/features/camera-details/utils/boundingBox.test.ts (new)

## TDD
RED: Tests for null input, all-zero box, partially non-zero box, fully non-zero box.
GREEN: Implement.

## Acceptance
- Function exists, exhaustive test coverage
- Spec ref: docs/specs/inline-event-clip-playback.md (Architecture > Helper)

title: Add bbox query param to snapshot proxy
description:
Update handleSnapshotRequest to accept bbox?: boolean and pass it to getEventSnapshot(eventId, { bbox }).

## Files
- src/features/camera-details/server/snapshot-proxy.ts
- src/features/camera-details/server/snapshot-proxy.test.ts (or wherever it lives)
- src/routes/api/events/$id/snapshot.ts (parse ?bbox=true from request URL)

## TDD
RED: Test that ?bbox=true causes getEventSnapshot to be called with { bbox: true }. Test that omitting it calls getEventSnapshot without bbox. Test that auth (401) and validation (400) still gate the request before any upstream call.
GREEN: Implement.

## Acceptance
- Spec AC11
- Spec ref: docs/specs/inline-event-clip-playback.md

title: Refactor clip proxy to serve inline + forward Range + support download flag
description:
Update handleClipRequest to:
1. Accept download?: boolean (default false). Only set Content-Disposition: attachment when true.
2. Accept rangeHeader?: string. Forward to the new getEventClipStream and propagate 206 + Content-Range back to the client.
3. Set Accept-Ranges: bytes on 200 responses.
4. Preserve existing 401 (unauth), 400 (invalid id), 502 (upstream failure) behavior.

## Files
- src/features/camera-details/server/clip-proxy.ts
- src/features/camera-details/server/clip-proxy.test.ts
- src/routes/api/events/$id/clip.ts (parse ?download=true and Range header from request)

## TDD
RED: Test each scenario from spec — inline default, ?download=true sets attachment, Range header returns 206, no Range returns 200 + Accept-Ranges, 401/400/502 unchanged.
GREEN: Implement.

## Depends on
- ce-mcx (streaming client)

## Acceptance
- Spec AC8, AC9, AC10
- Spec ref: docs/specs/inline-event-clip-playback.md

title: Add streaming Frigate client for event clips with Range support
description:
Replace or supplement getEventClip (which buffers the full mp4 into ArrayBuffer) with getEventClipStream that returns a streamable Response and forwards the Range header upstream.

## Files
- src/features/shared/server/frigate/client.ts (add getEventClipStream)
- src/features/shared/server/frigate/client.test.ts (extend tests)

## TDD
RED: Test that getEventClipStream returns a Response with status 206 and Content-Range when given a Range header. Test that it returns 200 with Accept-Ranges: bytes on full requests.
GREEN: Implement the streaming function using fetch with the Range header forwarded.

## Acceptance
- Spec AC9: Range request returns 206 + Content-Range
- No regression in mock-client.ts (add corresponding mock)
- Spec ref: docs/specs/inline-event-clip-playback.md

title: Inline event clip playback with bounding-box overlay
description:
Add inline video playback for event clips on the camera event detail page, plus a toggle to overlay Frigate's detection bounding box on the still snapshot.

## Why
Currently src/features/camera-details/components/EventSnapshot.tsx only renders the static snapshot.jpg, and clip support stops at download. Frigate already serves clip.mp4 (via getEventClip in src/features/shared/server/frigate/client.ts:181-188) and the snapshot endpoint accepts bbox=true + timestamp params (GetEventMediaParams in src/features/shared/server/frigate/types.ts:305-312).

Users currently have to open Frigate directly to see motion in context — this closes that gap.

## In scope
- HTML5 video player on the event detail page that streams the proxied clip with native controls (iOS-friendly)
- Bounding-box overlay toggle on the still snapshot (snapshot.jpg?bbox=true)
- Graceful fallback when has_clip is false
- Cross-platform: must work on iOS Safari standalone PWA and Android Chrome
- Maintain existing snapshot proxy auth pattern

## Out of scope
- Timeline scrubbing strip with keyframes (future epic)
- Multi-event clip stitching
- 24/7 recording playback

## Spec
Detailed Gherkin scenarios, architecture notes, and acceptance criteria live in docs/specs/inline-event-clip-playback/.

title: Cross-platform manual verification: iOS Safari PWA, Android Chrome, desktop
description:
Manual QA pass against AC1-AC4 and AC13 from the spec. Cannot be automated.

## Checklist
- [ ] iOS Safari standalone PWA: open event with clip, play inline (no fullscreen takeover)
- [ ] iOS Safari standalone PWA: scrub mid-clip, playback resumes from new position
- [ ] Android Chrome: open event with clip, play inline
- [ ] Desktop Chrome / Firefox / Safari: same
- [ ] Bounding-box toggle visually shows/hides the box on snapshot
- [ ] Lightbox preserves overlay state
- [ ] No SSR/hydration warnings in dev tools console
- [ ] Download link in InfoCard still triggers a file download

## Depends on
- ce-6pr (detail page wired)
- ce-j2v (clip proxy with Range)

## Acceptance
- Spec AC1, AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5, AC13
- Spec ref: docs/specs/inline-event-clip-playback.md

title: (Follow-up) Remove ?clip=inline opt-in flag after iOS verification
description:
Once ce-4pb confirms AC1-AC4 pass on a real iOS device, flip the default and remove the gate.

## Procedure
1. Replace useUrlFlag('clip', 'inline') with const showPlayer = true (or remove the wrapping conditional).
2. If useUrlFlag has no other caller, delete the hook file.
3. Update tests in CameraEventDetailPage.test.tsx that rely on the flag.

## Depends on
- ce-4pb (cross-platform manual QA passing)

## Spec
- AC21 (target state)
- Spec section 'Phased rollout' explicitly contemplates removal.
